output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for discogs_lastfm, discogs_musicbrainz, musicbrainz_lastfm, blocking on name, artist.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.94 to 0.96 and candidate counts range from 95134 to 450205.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.55, f1=0.8925619834710744, threshold=0.78, f1=0.8571428571428571, threshold=0.75); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to load three datasets and perform LLM-based schema matching to align lastfm and musicbrainz schemas to discogs.
EmbeddingBlocker was used for blocking with semantic similarity on specified columns and top_k=20.
RuleBasedMatcher was configured with specific comparators and weights for each dataset pair with defined thresholds.
Data fusion strategy was defined using longest_string fuser for seven attributes and union fuser for three track-related attributes.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion engine.
The fusion strategy favors the longest string for most textual attributes to prefer more complete values.
For track-related list attributes, the union fuser is used to combine all track information.
Matching behavior uses rule-based matching with weighted string, numeric, and date comparators tailored per dataset pair.
Schema matching uses an LLM-based matcher with a GPT-5.1 model and 10 rows for matching.
Blocking uses embedding-based blockers with the 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2' model and sklearn backend.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used in this run is a rule-based fusion strategy named 'rule_based_fusion_strategy'.
Evaluation functions were registered for core music-related fields including 'name', 'artist', 'duration', 'release-date', 'release-country', 'label', and 'tracks_track_name'.
Additional list-like columns detected in the data were evaluated using set equality match by default.
A helper function was added to parse source IDs from various formats.
A function was implemented to detect common gold ID prefixes to align fused clusters with gold IDs.
A build_eval_view function was created to align fused output with gold test set IDs, including fallback mechanisms.
List-like columns were detected and normalized to ensure consistent list comparisons during evaluation.
Coverage diagnostics were added to report direct and mapped ID coverage against the gold set.
Evaluation functions were registered explicitly for each relevant column using the DataFusionStrategy's add_evaluation_function method.
A DataFusionEvaluator was instantiated with debug options to evaluate the fused data against the gold standard and output structured metrics.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 64.96%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion strategy for the 'duration' attribute was changed from using 'longest_string' to using 'maximum'.
Singleton retention was disabled in the data fusion engine.
The fusion behavior shifted to treat the 'duration' attribute as a numeric value where the maximum value is preferred, rather than the longest string representation.
This change reflects a more conservative approach to aggregating the total duration of releases.
Normalization and parsing functions were added to clean and standardize list-like track columns and numeric duration values before matching and fusion.
The pipeline ensured all needed columns exist in each dataset after schema matching and renaming.
Correspondences are saved with correct filenames after matching.
The fusion strategy continues to use 'longest_string' for most textual attributes and 'union' for track-related list attributes.

Changing the fusion of 'duration' to 'maximum' makes sense because duration is a numeric attribute representing total seconds, and taking the maximum is a conservative and meaningful aggregation for total release duration.

Normalization functions were improved to handle missing values more robustly and to avoid errors on array-like inputs.
Helper columns for embedding blocking were added to improve semantic blocking robustness.
The pipeline continues to use rule-based matching with string and numeric comparators and embedding-based blocking.
The fusion strategy remains the same, predominantly using longest_string for most attributes, union for track-related list attributes, and maximum for duration.
Singleton retention is disabled, meaning singletons are excluded from the fusion output.

This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline following an evaluation that revealed weak fusion behavior.
The fusion strategy was adjusted to use 'prefer_non_empty_string' for the attributes release-date, release-country, genre, and label instead of 'longest_string'.
The fusion method for tracks_track_position was changed from 'union' to 'fuse_track_positions'.
The fusion method for tracks_track_name was changed from 'union' to 'fuse_track_names'.
The fusion method for tracks_track_duration was changed from 'union' to 'fuse_track_durations'.
Singleton retention was disabled in this iteration.
The fusion behavior shifted towards preferring non-empty strings for several attributes, moving away from simply choosing the longest string.
Track-related attributes now use specialized fusion functions instead of union, indicating a more nuanced approach to merging track data.
Embedding-based blocking was introduced using normalized helper columns for name and artist to improve blocking quality.
Rule-based matching and string comparison support were maintained and integrated with the updated blocking and fusion strategies.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a rule-based fusion strategy with attribute-specific evaluation functions.
Core scalar fields such as name, artist, release-country, and label were evaluated using tokenized_match.
Duration was evaluated using numeric_tolerance_match.
Release-date was evaluated using year_only_match.
Track-related fields including tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration were evaluated using set_equality_match.
Any other detected list-like columns were also evaluated using set_equality_match.
Added optional list normalization to ensure consistent comparison of list-like columns between fused and gold data.
Improved alignment of fused cluster IDs to gold IDs by expanding fused clusters into rows keyed by likely corresponding gold IDs using _fusion_sources and dominant gold prefixes.
Updated evaluation function registration to use the correct API method add_evaluation_function.
Enhanced coverage diagnostics to report direct and mapped ID coverage.
Refined the evaluation output handling to write results to a fixed output file path.

This run was a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation results.
The fusion strategy for the 'name' attribute was changed from 'prefer_non_empty_string' to 'prefer_title_without_artist_prefix'.
The fusion strategy for the 'artist' attribute was changed from 'prefer_non_empty_string' to 'prefer_artist_field'.
The fusion strategy for the 'release-country' attribute was changed from 'prefer_non_empty_string' to 'fuse_release_country'.
Singleton retention was disabled in this iteration.

The fusion behavior shifted to more specialized fusers for key attributes: 'name' now prefers titles without embedded artist prefixes, 'artist' prefers the artist field explicitly, and 'release-country' uses a dedicated fusion function to canonicalize country names.
Overall, fusion moved away from a generic 'prefer_non_empty_string' approach for these attributes to more domain-aware fusion strategies.
Normalization of 'release-country' was added to canonicalize common country variants to improve fusion accuracy.
Track-related attributes continue to use specialized fusion functions such as 'fuse_track_names', 'fuse_track_positions', and 'fuse_track_durations'.

This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used in this run was the music_fusion_strategy.

Core columns such as name, artist, duration, release-date, release-country, label, and tracks_track_name were evaluated using tokenized_match, numeric_tolerance_match, year_only_match, and set_equality_match functions.
Any detected list-like columns not explicitly registered were evaluated using set_equality_match.

The evaluation view was improved by mapping fused clusters to gold IDs via _fusion_sources when present, preferring source IDs that match the gold ID namespace or exact ID overlap, with fallback to cluster ID.
List-like columns were detected and normalized consistently between fused and gold datasets.
Evaluation functions were registered explicitly for core columns and list-like columns.
Coverage diagnostics were added to help interpret scores.
Debugging output was enabled with JSON debug files.
The evaluation refinement was performed to better align fused clusters to gold IDs for a fair per-record evaluation.
Normalization of list-like columns ensures consistent comparison between fused and gold data.
Using the music_fusion_strategy matches the fusion domain and test set characteristics.
